result:
The pivot experiment is registered. Summary of what I designed:

**The pivot**: the flat `nn.Linear(560, 1024)` map-cell classifier — 1,024 completely independent weight rows, unchanged in kind since exp 2/10 — is replaced with a coordinate-conditioned generator: a tiny shared MLP takes each cell's Fourier-encoded (u,v) position and produces its embedding, scored against a metric-learned crop embedding via cosine similarity. This directly targets the diagnosed "train-split and eval-split medians are equal" signature (exp 26's own named symptom of a statistically weak parameterization) via a mechanism orthogonal to why the one prior parameterization attempt (exp 31, neural-map correlation field) failed — that one used a spatial cross-correlation kernel sensitive to residual heading error; this one compares two flat vectors, exactly like today's head, so it isn't exposed to that failure mode.

As a structural consequence, every previously-frozen stage changes: the feature extractor now also taps the trunk's stride-8 block, the two-expert brightness gate is replaced by continuous FiLM conditioning, decode/probability-map operate on the new similarity field, and confidence reads an embedding-space peak-margin instead of hand-crafted field-shape features. Only one line of `train.py` changes (the trunk-parameter reference); the sampler, calibration, and schedule are untouched.

I validated the architecture figure against `figcheck` (PASS) and checked internal consistency of the implementation brief (a dimension mismatch in the confidence head that I caught and fixed). `runs/pending_experiment.json` is ready for the harness to hand to the implementation agent.

tool_input.command: wc -l model/model.py model/train.py 2>/dev/null; echo "---"; sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null | head -100
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" > /tmp/last_arch.json; cat /tmp/last_arch.json | python3 -m json.tool | head -150
sqlite3 experiments.sqlite "SELECT arch_json FROM experiments WHERE arch_json IS NOT NULL ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1;" > /tmp/last_arch.json
